findings.title: Travel CSV import accepts spreadsheet-formula payloads and persists them into exportable fields
findings.exploit: An Ops user can import travel rows whose free-text cells start with formula characters such as '=' or '@'. The importer stores those strings unchanged, and the Excel export path later writes the same cells into workbook rows without any formula neutralization.

tests_run.command: npx vitest run src/lib/actions/travel-import.test.ts -t 'rejects spreadsheet formulas in imported text fields'

failure_evidence: The new regression test expected the batch to reject a formula-prefixed fromCity value.
Current behavior imported the row successfully instead, so result.imported was 1 rather than 0.

findings.title: Accommodation CSV import accepts spreadsheet-formula payloads and persists them into exportable fields
findings.exploit: An Ops user can import accommodation rows whose hotelName or other free-text cells begin with spreadsheet formula prefixes. The importer inserts those values unchanged, and the rooming-list Excel export later emits the same strings without sanitization.

tests_run.command: npx vitest run src/lib/actions/accommodation-import.test.ts -t 'rejects spreadsheet formulas in imported text fields'

failure_evidence: The new regression test expected a formula-prefixed hotelName to be rejected before insert.
Current behavior imported the row successfully instead, so result.imported was 1 rather than 0.

findings.title: Global security headers omit Content-Security-Policy
findings.exploit: The deployment config sets several defensive headers but does not publish a Content-Security-Policy at all, leaving the app without a browser-enforced script and resource allowlist.
failure_evidence: The test enumerated configured headers from vercel.json and expected Content-Security-Policy to be present.
Current configured keys were X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, X-DNS-Prefetch-Control, Strict-Transport-Security, and Permissions-Policy only.

no_fresh_issue_confirmed.status: no fresh issue confirmed on the reviewed surface
no_fresh_issue_confirmed.notes: Notification provider credentials are read from environment variables rather than hardcoded in src/, and certificate verification tokens are UUID defaults generated by the database schema rather than weak application-side randomness.

no_fresh_issue_confirmed.status: no fresh issue confirmed on the reviewed travel/accommodation/transport mutation paths
no_fresh_issue_confirmed.notes: The inspected create/update/cancel/import/suggestion mutation paths all write audit records, and the reviewed audit meta payloads carried IDs, statuses, and workflow metadata rather than direct email, phone, or full-name fields.

no_fresh_issue_confirmed.status: no fresh exploit confirmed on the reviewed fetch callsites
no_fresh_issue_confirmed.notes: On the reviewed surface, fetch targets were derived from provider/storage configuration and signed-object lookups rather than user-submitted or database-stored arbitrary URLs.
